text:
第 1 页：Title

流程脚本（中文动作 + 英文口播）：
步骤 1｜动作（中文）：停留在封面页，不切换窗口。先把鼠标放在标题区域附近。
说（英文）：Today I will present our course project, a retrieval-augmented knowledge QA agent for single-cell and spatial omics. The key motivation is that modern large language models are fluent, but fluency is not enough for specialized biological question answering. In this domain, an answer should not only sound reasonable. It should be connected to papers, textbook materials, and trusted biological databases.

步骤 2｜动作（中文）：鼠标依次指向右侧三个数字：28、4696、30。
说（英文）：So this project does not train a new large language model from scratch. Instead, it builds a knowledge and tool layer around an existing LLM. The current system contains 28 knowledge sets, 4,696 indexed chunks, and a 30-question benchmark. These numbers matter because they show that this is not just a single demo prompt. It is a small but complete domain QA system with data assets, persistent storage, evaluation questions, and visible module behavior.

步骤 3｜动作（中文）：鼠标回到标题下方，准备切到下一页。
说（英文）：On top of the basic RAG workflow, I added structured retrieval, agentic multi-step planning, persistent memory, and a UniProt protein skill. Next I will summarize what has actually been delivered in the project.

text:
第 2 页：What I Built

流程脚本（中文动作 + 英文口播）：
步骤 1｜动作（中文）：停留在 PPT。鼠标指向左上角 Knowledge Assets 模块。
说（英文）：This slide summarizes what was actually built in the project. The first deliverable is the knowledge asset layer. Instead of simply putting PDFs and books into a folder, each source is organized into a knowledge-set structure. The source content, metadata, and keywords are stored separately, so later retrieval and citation can be traced back to real materials.

步骤 2｜动作（中文）：鼠标指向右上角 Searchable Index 模块。
说（英文）：The second deliverable is the searchable index. The system extracts text from PDF, EPUB, TXT, and Markdown files. After extraction, the text is split into overlapping chunks, embedded with the bge-m3 model, and stored locally as reusable vector data. This means the runtime system does not need to read every book and paper again whenever the web app starts.

步骤 3｜动作（中文）：鼠标指向左下角 Evaluation Benchmark 模块。
说（英文）：The third deliverable is the benchmark. I did not want the project to be judged only by a few examples that happened to work. The benchmark contains 30 questions, and each question includes expected answer points, required sources, and failure constraints.

步骤 4｜动作（中文）：鼠标指向右下角 Enhanced QA Modules 模块，然后指向底部总结句。
说（英文）：The fourth deliverable is the enhanced QA system itself. Structured KB, Agentic RAG, memory, and UniProt Skill are not isolated decorations. They are connected into a collaborative QA route. In other words, this project covers the full chain: source organization, index construction, retrieval, answer generation, tool use, memory, and evaluation. Now I will show how these deliverables are connected in the actual system architecture.

name: architecture-table

text:
第 3 页：System Architecture

流程脚本（中文动作 + 英文口播）：
步骤 1｜动作（中文）：先不要切浏览器。鼠标沿着 PPT 中 Curate sources -> Build index -> Retrieve evidence -> Generate answer -> Write memory 的流程移动。
说（英文）：This architecture slide explains why the system is more than a direct call to an LLM. In a normal chat system, the user question is sent almost directly to the language model, and the model answers from its internal parameters. In this project, the user question first enters a pipeline that checks documents, source metadata, keywords, vector indexes, structured records, and memory.

步骤 2｜动作（中文）：鼠标指向表格里的 Reasoning layer。
说（英文）：Then the system decides how to retrieve evidence. For a simple factual question, ordinary vector retrieval may be enough. For a question with hard constraints, the structured knowledge base can narrow down candidate sources before vector search. For comparison or multi-part questions, the agentic planner can split the problem into several search steps.

步骤 3｜动作（中文）：此时切到 PyCharm。如果服务没有启动，就运行 scripts/rag_web.py；如果已经启动，直接切到浏览器。
说（英文）：I will briefly show that this is a running system, not only a diagram. The web app is started from scripts/rag_web.py, and the browser interface is the entry point for the demo.

步骤 4｜动作（中文）：浏览器打开 http://127.0.0.1:8000，指向页面顶部状态栏里的 embedding、llm、chunks。
说（英文）：Here the status bar shows the loaded embedding model, the configured LLM, and the number of chunks available for retrieval. This proves that the backend has loaded the evidence layer before we ask questions.

步骤 5｜动作（中文）：再打开 http://127.0.0.1:8000/health，指向 chunk_count、embedding_model、llm_model、structured_kb、memory。
说（英文）：The health endpoint gives a more technical view. It shows the vector store, structured knowledge base, LLM configuration, and memory status. Next I will describe the knowledge assets that feed this pipeline.

text:
第 4 页：Knowledge Assets

流程脚本（中文动作 + 英文口播）：
步骤 1｜动作（中文）：回到第 4 页 PPT，鼠标指向 28 valid knowledge sets。
说（英文）：The knowledge asset layer is the foundation of the whole system. In a RAG project, retrieval quality is strongly limited by the quality and structure of the knowledge base. If the sources are messy, duplicated, or poorly described, the generated answer will also be hard to trust.

步骤 2｜动作（中文）：鼠标指向页面中间的要点，尤其是 content、source metadata、keywords。
说（英文）：Each knowledge set contains three main parts. The content folder stores readable material, such as extracted paper text or book text. The source.json file records source-level metadata, including title, authors, DOI, URL, and source type. The keywords.json file gives a lightweight semantic description of the material, including topics, methods, organisms, and related terms.

步骤 3｜动作（中文）：可选演示。切到 PyCharm，打开 data/01-academic/nicheformer-foundation-model-single-cell-spatial-omics-2025，依次展开 content、source.json、keywords.json。如果时间紧，就不切窗口，只继续讲。
说（英文）：These files are not labels for supervised training. They are used for indexing, filtering, retrieval explanation, and citation tracking. The goal is to make every answer traceable back to a real source.

步骤 4｜动作（中文）：如果已经打开 source.json，就指向 title、doi 或 url；再打开 keywords.json，指向主题词。
说（英文）：The current topic focus is single-cell omics, spatial omics, multi-omics, foundation models, tissue architecture, cell state analysis, and related biological methods. I also included both recent English papers and Chinese textbook-style materials. The recent papers provide frontier methods, while the books help answer background biological questions. After the sources are organized, the next step is to make them searchable.

name: footer
text: Single-cell and Spatial Omics Knowledge QA Agent
textPreview: Single-cell and Spatial Omics Knowledge QA Agent

text:
第 5 页：Index Building

流程脚本（中文动作 + 英文口播）：
步骤 1｜动作（中文）：回到第 5 页 PPT，鼠标依次指向 Extract、Chunk、Embed 三个模块。
说（英文）：After the knowledge assets are organized, the next problem is how to make them searchable. A book or paper is usually too long to send directly to an LLM, so the system first extracts text from the source format. It supports PDF, EPUB, TXT, and Markdown, because the knowledge assets include both research papers and books.

步骤 2｜动作（中文）：鼠标指向 Chunk 模块。
说（英文）：After text extraction, the document is split into overlapping chunks. The chunking strategy is not perfect semantic segmentation, but it is practical. The goal is to keep each chunk small enough for retrieval and prompt construction, while still preserving enough local context for the LLM to understand the meaning. The overlap reduces the chance that a key sentence is separated from the explanation around it.

步骤 3｜动作（中文）：切到 PyCharm，打开 storage/vector_store_bge_m3/manifest.json，指向 embedding_model、chunk_count、dimension。
说（英文）：Then the system calls the bge-m3 embedding model through Ollama and creates a vector representation for each chunk. In this version, the vector dimension is 1024, and the final index contains 4,696 chunks. The manifest records these settings, so the index is reproducible.

步骤 4｜动作（中文）：在同一文件夹里指向 chunks.jsonl、vectors.npy、manifest.json；不要现场运行 build_index。
说（英文）：The vector store is not temporary memory. It is persisted under storage/vector_store_bge_m3, together with chunks.jsonl, vectors.npy, and manifest.json. Once the index is built, the runtime system can load it directly. That makes the project easier to demonstrate and easier to compare later when we test different retrieval strategies. Once the index exists, we need a benchmark to test whether retrieval and answering are reliable.

title: Single-cell and Spatial Omics RAG Agent

text:
第 6 页：Benchmark Design

流程脚本（中文动作 + 英文口播）：
步骤 1｜动作（中文）：回到第 6 页 PPT，鼠标指向表格里的四类测试目标。
说（英文）：The benchmark is one of the most important parts of the project because RAG systems can fail in ways that are not obvious from a single demo. A system may retrieve the wrong source, answer without support, cite irrelevant evidence, fail to synthesize multiple papers, or ignore a module that we expect it to use.

步骤 2｜动作（中文）：切到 PyCharm，打开 data/benchmark/single_cell_omics_rag_benchmark_30.json。用搜索找到 sc-rag-001，展示 question、required_knowledge_set_ids、expected_answer_points、must_not_claim。
说（英文）：For this reason, the benchmark contains more than just a list of questions. Each item includes the question type, difficulty, required knowledge-set IDs, expected answer points, must-not-claim constraints, retrieval expectations, and target modules. These fields make evaluation more concrete.

步骤 3｜动作（中文）：切到网页 http://127.0.0.1:8000，在输入框粘贴：Nicheformer 这篇论文主要解决了单细胞和空间组学中的什么问题？它为什么需要同时利用解离单细胞数据和空间转录组数据？然后点击 问答。
说（英文）：Now I will run one benchmark-style question using the ordinary QA route. The important thing is not only whether the final answer sounds fluent, but whether the retrieved evidence comes from the expected Nicheformer source.

步骤 4｜动作（中文）：等待回答完成。指向右侧证据片段、回答里的引用、Nicheformer 相关来源。
说（英文）：Here we can inspect the evidence, the citation behavior, and whether the answer covers the expected points. This is why the benchmark turns the project from a subjective demonstration into something closer to an evaluable system. Next I will show how structured metadata improves retrieval beyond pure vector similarity.

title: Single-cell and Spatial Omics RAG Agent

name: footer
text: Single-cell and Spatial Omics Knowledge QA Agent
textPreview: Single-cell and Spatial Omics Knowledge QA Agent

text:
第 7 页：Structured KB

流程脚本（中文动作 + 英文口播）：
步骤 1｜动作（中文）：回到第 7 页 PPT，鼠标指向顶部说明句和 Question -> structured candidate filtering -> vector search 的流程。
说（英文）：Structured KB addresses a weakness of pure vector search. Vector search is good at semantic similarity, but it is not naturally reliable for hard constraints. If a user asks for papers from a certain year, method family, or source type, vector similarity alone may return text that sounds similar but violates the constraint.

步骤 2｜动作（中文）：鼠标指向 28 paper/book records、316 author entries、111 keywords、24/14 themes/methods。
说（英文）：To solve this, I built a structured SQLite knowledge base from the metadata. It stores records such as papers, books, authors, keywords, themes, methods, publication years, source types, and knowledge-set IDs. This gives the system a way to reason over explicit fields before doing semantic retrieval.

步骤 3｜动作（中文）：切到网页，在输入框粘贴：请找出 2026 年左右与单细胞或空间组学 foundation model 相关的研究，并说明它们关注的问题。点击 Hybrid 问答。
说（英文）：Now I will use Hybrid RAG. This question contains metadata constraints, especially the approximate year and the foundation-model topic. The system should first narrow the candidate sources using structured metadata, and then run vector retrieval inside that smaller candidate pool.

步骤 4｜动作（中文）：等待回答完成。指向检索决策过程里的 Structured candidates、matched_by、year、keyword 或 theme，再指向右侧证据片段。
说（英文）：In the trace, we can see why certain candidate sources were selected. This improves explainability and reduces the chance that a semantically similar but irrelevant source dominates the answer. Structured KB is not a replacement for vector search. It is a control layer before vector search. The next module goes one step further by letting the LLM plan several retrieval steps.

title: Single-cell and Spatial Omics RAG Agent

name: section
text: Single-cell and Spatial Omics RAG Agent
textPreview: Single-cell and Spatial Omics RAG Agent

name: slide-title
text: Agentic RAG: Plan First, Retrieve in Steps
textPreview: Agentic RAG: Plan First, Retrieve in Steps

name: intro
text: Basic RAG usually retrieves once. Agentic RAG asks the LLM to produce a retrieval plan, then the program executes each step.
textPreview: Basic RAG usually retrieves once. Agentic RAG asks the LLM to produce a retrieval plan, then the program executes each step.

name: case
text: Typical gain: for Nicheformer versus CONCORD, the agent searches both targets separately before comparing them.
textPreview: Typical gain: for Nicheformer versus CONCORD, the agent searches both targets separately before comparing them.

name: footer
text: Single-cell and Spatial Omics Knowledge QA Agent
textPreview: Single-cell and Spatial Omics Knowledge QA Agent

text:
第 8 页：Agentic RAG

流程脚本（中文动作 + 英文口播）：
步骤 1｜动作（中文）：回到第 8 页 PPT，鼠标指向 Planner、Stepwise retrieval、Evidence merge、Fallback 四行。
说（英文）：Agentic RAG is designed for questions that should not be answered with one single retrieval query. In basic RAG, the system embeds the whole user question, retrieves the top chunks, and answers from them. That workflow is simple, but it can be unbalanced for comparison questions.

步骤 2｜动作（中文）：鼠标指向页面底部 Nicheformer versus CONCORD 的句子。
说（英文）：For example, if the user asks to compare Nicheformer and CONCORD, one retrieval query may retrieve mostly Nicheformer evidence and miss CONCORD, or it may retrieve general background text without enough method-specific detail.

步骤 3｜动作（中文）：切到网页，在输入框粘贴：比较 Nicheformer 和 CONCORD：它们分别解决什么问题，核心思路有什么不同？点击 Agentic 问答。
说（英文）：Now I will use the Agentic RAG route. The LLM first acts as a planner. It does not directly produce the final answer at this stage. Instead, it returns a small JSON plan with the question type, reasoning, and several retrieval steps.

步骤 4｜动作（中文）：等待回答完成。指向检索决策过程中的 question_type、reasoning、Step 1、Step 2、每个 step 的 query 和 results。
说（英文）：The program executes these retrieval steps, gathers evidence for each step, deduplicates repeated chunks, and merges the evidence into the final answer prompt. The advantage is that we can inspect whether the answer is balanced. If it fails, we can also see whether the failure came from planning, retrieval, or final generation. After planning, another important issue is memory across turns.

name: footer
text: Single-cell and Spatial Omics Knowledge QA Agent
textPreview: Single-cell and Spatial Omics Knowledge QA Agent

text:
第 9 页：Persistent Memory

流程脚本（中文动作 + 英文口播）：
步骤 1｜动作（中文）：回到第 9 页 PPT，鼠标指向 Working Memory、Episodic Memory、Semantic Memory 三个模块。
说（英文）：The memory system is included because a useful QA agent should not treat every turn as completely isolated. In many real learning or research scenarios, the user gives preferences over time. Later, the user may ask a short follow-up question, and without memory that question is underspecified.

步骤 2｜动作（中文）：鼠标指向底部 Before answering: search_memory() 和 After answering: record_interaction()。
说（英文）：In this project, memory has three layers. Working memory stores recent messages in the current session. Episodic memory stores summaries of previous interactions. Semantic memory stores reusable relationships as triples. The system searches memory before answering and writes memory after answering.

步骤 3｜动作（中文）：切到网页，在输入框粘贴：我后面主要关注空间转录组质量控制和平台偏差问题，请记住这个偏好。点击 协作问答。
说（英文）：For the first turn, I give the system a preference. I tell it that my later focus is spatial transcriptomics quality control and platform bias. This should be stored as useful interaction context.

步骤 4｜动作（中文）：等待回答完成后，在输入框粘贴：根据我刚才的偏好，我应该优先阅读哪些资料，为什么？再次点击 协作问答。
说（英文）：For the second turn, I ask a short follow-up question. The system should connect this question to my previous preference, search memory, and then still use the local knowledge base for evidence-based recommendations.

步骤 5｜动作（中文）：指向回答中提到偏好的位置，或者指向 trace / memory stats 里的 memory 相关内容。如果现场时间不够，只展示 /health 或 memory stats 中 working、episodic、semantic 数量。
说（英文）：This shows that memory is cooperating with RAG instead of replacing it. The answer should use the remembered preference and still remain grounded in retrieved materials. The next module connects biological entity mentions to an external authoritative database.

name: points
text:
- UniProt is public and does not require an API key for this use case.
- The skill is integrated into collaborative QA, not only exposed as a standalone button.
- Entity detection is conservative: alias table, regex, and method-name filtering.
textPreview: - UniProt is public and does not require an API key for this use case. | - The skill is integrated into collaborative QA, not only expose...

name: footer
text: Single-cell and Spatial Omics Knowledge QA Agent
textPreview: Single-cell and Spatial Omics Knowledge QA Agent

text:
第 10 页：UniProt Skill

流程脚本（中文动作 + 英文口播）：
步骤 1｜动作（中文）：回到第 10 页 PPT，鼠标指向 OCT4 和 TP53/P53 两行。
说（英文）：The UniProt Skill addresses biological entity ambiguity. In biomedical text, a symbol such as OCT4 or TP53 can refer to a gene, a protein, an alias, or a biological role depending on context. A general LLM may know something about these entities, but it may not provide a standardized database entry.

步骤 2｜动作（中文）：鼠标指向页面下方三条说明：public、integrated、conservative entity detection。
说（英文）：The UniProt Skill is a local tool wrapper around the public UniProt REST API. For this use case, it does not require an API key. When the question contains a protein or gene mention, the system can call the skill and return structured information such as accession, recommended protein name, organism, function summary, and link.

步骤 3｜动作（中文）：切到网页，在输入框粘贴：OCT4 在细胞重编程中是什么角色？请查 UniProt。点击 UniProt Skill。
说（英文）：Now I will call the skill directly. This is the cleanest way to show entity normalization. For OCT4, the expected reviewed human UniProt entry is Q01860.

步骤 4｜动作（中文）：等待结果。指向 selected_skill、selected_tool、selected_accession 或 accession Q01860。如果失败或网络慢，改用备用问题：TP53/P53 的标准人类蛋白条目是什么？预期 P04637。
说（英文）：Here the result is not just generated text. It is a structured external database result. This reduces hallucination and helps the answer distinguish between local biological context and standardized protein information.

步骤 5｜动作（中文）：保留 OCT4 问题或重新粘贴同一问题，点击 协作问答。指向 trace 里的 UniProt Skill calls，以及回答中的 accession / UniProt 链接。
说（英文）：The skill is also integrated into collaborative QA. That means the final system can combine local RAG evidence with the UniProt record. Now I will combine these modules into the final collaborative QA route.

text: The final route is not a stack of unrelated buttons. It is one chain where memory, planning, structured filtering, vector evidence, and UniProt work together.
textPreview: The final route is not a stack of unrelated buttons. It is one chain where memory, planning, structured filtering, vector evidence, and U...

name: footer
text: Single-cell and Spatial Omics Knowledge QA Agent
textPreview: Single-cell and Spatial Omics Knowledge QA Agent

text:
第 11 页：Integrated Mode

流程脚本（中文动作 + 英文口播）：
步骤 1｜动作（中文）：回到第 11 页 PPT，鼠标沿着 Memory -> Plan -> Filter -> Retrieve -> Skill -> Answer 的流程移动。
说（英文）：This is the final system view. The standalone buttons are useful for ablation, because they let us compare basic retrieval, retrieve-only behavior, agentic retrieval, hybrid retrieval, and tool use separately. However, the real system is the collaborative QA route, because real questions often require more than one capability at the same time.

步骤 2｜动作（中文）：鼠标指向下方表格，强调 standalone entry 是消融入口，Collaborative QA 是最终入口。
说（英文）：In the collaborative route, the system first searches memory, then uses the agentic planner to decide retrieval steps. For each step, structured metadata can filter candidate knowledge sets before vector retrieval. If the question contains a protein or gene entity, the UniProt Skill is called and added to the same evidence package. Finally, the LLM generates an answer under citation constraints and the interaction is written back into memory.

步骤 3｜动作（中文）：切到网页，在输入框粘贴：请结合本地资料和 UniProt，说明 OCT4 在细胞重编程中的作用，并指出回答依据来自哪里。点击 协作问答。
说（英文）：Now I will use the integrated route. This question is intentionally designed to require both local knowledge and external protein normalization. Local sources should provide the biological explanation, while UniProt should provide the standardized entity information.

步骤 4｜动作（中文）：等待回答完成。依次指向 trace 中的 memory、plan、structured candidates、retrieval results、UniProt Skill calls；再指向右侧证据片段；最后指向回答中区分本地文献和 UniProt 的句子。
说（英文）：The trace is the key to making this believable. Instead of only showing the final answer, the interface shows memory hits, planning, structured candidates, retrieval results, and tool calls. That means the audience can inspect how the answer was constructed. The last slide gives a practical demonstration route for the defense.

name: final
text: In one sentence: this is a runnable, traceable, and evaluable RAG Agent built around single-cell and spatial omics knowledge assets.
textPreview: In one sentence: this is a runnable, traceable, and evaluable RAG Agent built around single-cell and spatial omics knowledge assets.

name: footer
text: Single-cell and Spatial Omics Knowledge QA Agent
textPreview: Single-cell and Spatial Omics Knowledge QA Agent

text:
第 12 页：Live Demo Route and Takeaways

流程脚本（中文动作 + 英文口播）：
步骤 1｜动作（中文）：回到最后一页 PPT，鼠标指向左侧 Recommended demo order。
说（英文）：To close the presentation, I summarize the live demonstration route. The purpose of the demo is not to show every line of code. The purpose is to prove that the system is runnable, traceable, and evaluable.

步骤 2｜动作（中文）：指向第一条 Open /health。
说（英文）：First, I open the health endpoint to show that the vector store, structured knowledge base, LLM configuration, embedding model, and memory database are loaded. This proves that the following answers are coming from the project system rather than from a static slide.

步骤 3｜动作（中文）：指向第二条和第三条。如果前面已经演示过，就说这是正式答辩的固定顺序。
说（英文）：Second, I run one benchmark question about Nicheformer using the ordinary QA button. Third, I run a comparison question using Agentic RAG, such as comparing Nicheformer and CONCORD. In both cases, the important part is not only the final answer, but the evidence and trace behind it.

步骤 4｜动作（中文）：指向第四条和第五条。
说（英文）：Fourth, I run a metadata-constrained question using Hybrid RAG. Fifth, I ask an OCT4 or TP53 question to demonstrate UniProt normalization. If time allows, I also run the two-turn memory demo: first tell the system a research preference, then ask for reading recommendations based on that preference.

步骤 5｜动作（中文）：指向右侧 Takeaways，再指向底部总结句。
说（英文）：The main takeaway is that this project is not a static report. It is a working RAG agent with organized knowledge assets, persistent vector storage, a structured knowledge base, agentic planning, memory, external tool use, and a benchmark for evaluation. The final system is still a course project, so it is not a production biomedical assistant, but it demonstrates the core engineering ideas behind domain-specific retrieval-augmented agents. Future work can compare retrieval strategies and models using the same benchmark.
